advisor_sources.Integrator_Detailed: INTEGRATE-findings-summary.yaml (4 blocking findings: F-INTEGRATE-1 through F-INTEGRATE-4)

Initiative-1-CRITICAL.title: Cryptographic Cursor Integrity & Signing
merged_from: F-S-1 (Sentinel: Cursor Injection via Direct Map Manipulation)
Initiative-1-CRITICAL.impact: Event skipping, escalation bypass, Genesis polling unable to resume safely
Initiative-1-CRITICAL.business_risk: CRITICAL - Genesis coordination fails on bridge restart
Initiative-1-CRITICAL.description: Cursor state stored in unprotected global Map. Attacker can manipulate cursor.eventIndex to skip events. No HMAC/signature verification. Genesis has no way to detect stale cursors after bridge restart.

option_A_full.title: HMAC-SHA256 signing + cursor lifecycle signals
option_A_full.description: Sign cursors with HMAC. Add cursor_valid and age_seconds to readMessages() response. Genesis polling checks cursor validity before using.

option_B_pragmatic.title: Basic cursor age tracking (defer crypto)
option_B_pragmatic.description: Add cursor creation timestamp, return age in readMessages(). Genesis detects stale (>24h) cursors, resets. No HMAC yet.

Initiative-1-CRITICAL.recommended_approach: option_A_full - Cannot defer cryptographic integrity for Genesis

Initiative-2-CRITICAL.title: Path Traversal & Symlink Resolution Hardening
merged_from: F-A-7 (API Architect: Missing Documentation on Project ID Format and Path Safety)
Initiative-2-CRITICAL.impact: Arbitrary file read/write via symlink escape, path traversal
Initiative-2-CRITICAL.business_risk: CRITICAL - Security bypass, data exfiltration risk
Initiative-2-CRITICAL.description: resolveProjectPath() uses path.normalize() and prefix check but NOT realpathSync(). Symlinks bypass checks. POST /api/projects/:id accepts user-controlled projectId with no validation before path operations.

fixes_required.fix: Use realpathSync() BEFORE prefix check in resolveProjectPath()
Reject path separators and .. in projectId at HTTP handler layer

Initiative-3-CRITICAL.title: File Locking & Race Condition Mitigation (Manifest & Cursor Persistence)
merged_from: F-S-3 (Sentinel: Race Condition in saveManifest() Lock Mechanism)
F-R-1, F-R-2, F-R-5 (Pragmatist Synthesis: Concurrency Races in Polling & Copier)
Initiative-3-CRITICAL.consensus: 3/4 FIX_NOW (Sentinel demands atomic, Pragmatist accepts flag-based)
Initiative-3-CRITICAL.impact: Corrupted manifest.yaml, lost methodology entries, manifest truncation
Initiative-3-CRITICAL.business_risk: HIGH - Data loss, registry corruption, cascading failures
Initiative-3-CRITICAL.description: Advisory lock in saveManifest() is TOCTOU-vulnerable. Both saveManifest() and polling loop read/modify/write cursors without synchronization. File write can be interleaved.

option_A_atomic.title: Atomic file operations (fs.open with 'wx', or proper-lockfile library)
option_A_atomic.description: Use fs.open(..., 'wx') for exclusive creation, or adopt proper-lockfile npm package. Ensures atomic writes.

option_B_pragmatic_flag.title: Simple flag-based locking (skip races, not prevent)
option_B_pragmatic_flag.description: Add _pollLocked flag to GenesisPollingLoop. If already locked, skip this poll cycle. Does not prevent all races but catches 80% of contention.

Initiative-3-CRITICAL.phase_2_upgrade: Phase 3 - Use async-lock for proper mutex

Initiative-4-HIGH.title: Genesis Tool Registration & MCP Wiring (Polling Loop Instantiation)
merged_from: F-A-1 (Integrator Architecture: Genesis tools not registered in MCP)
F-A-3 (Integrator Architecture: Polling loop never instantiated)
F-INTEGRATE-4 (Integrator: Explicit Project Discovery MCP Tools Not Yet Defined)
Initiative-4-HIGH.impact: Genesis cannot observe, coordinate, or report. Phase 2 feature is non-functional.
Initiative-4-HIGH.description: Five Genesis MCP tools are implemented in code but (1) not registered in MCP ListToolsRequestSchema, (2) not wired to CallToolRequestSchema handlers, (3) not exposed as bridge HTTP routes, (4) polling loop never instantiated at startup. Genesis tools are dead code.

required_changes.task: Register Genesis tools in MCP index.ts
Add MCP CallToolRequestSchema handlers for 5 tools
Create bridge routes for Genesis tool HTTP exposure
Add session identity enforcement (genesis_report requires session.id === 'genesis-root')

Initiative-5-CRITICAL.title: Event Durability & Persistence (Genesis Events YAML)
merged_from: F-RISKM-2 (Integrator: Event log wiped on bridge restart)
F-ARCHI-1 (Integrator: Event durability gap breaks Genesis foundation)
F-INTEGRATE-1 (Integrator: Event Type Extensibility Path Missing)
Initiative-5-CRITICAL.impact: Audit loss, Genesis failure on restart, compliance risk, Phase 2 Genesis cannot function
Initiative-5-CRITICAL.business_risk: CRITICAL - Genesis requires event history for coordination
Initiative-5-CRITICAL.description: Phase 1 spec says 'events not preserved across restart'. Phase 2 Genesis depends on persistent history. Consensus from 3 advisors: event durability must be Phase 1, non-negotiable.

features: Extend ProjectEventType enum with Genesis event types (GENESIS_INITIALIZED, GENESIS_TASK_COMPLETED, GENESIS_ESCALATION, etc.)

also_addresses: F-P-1 (Event log unbounded growth) - cap at 100K entries in-memory
F-P-2 (Cursor cleanup) - implement 7-day TTL on unused cursors
F-INTEGRATE-1 (Event type extensibility) - extend ProjectEventType enum

Initiative-6-MEDIUM.title: Input Validation & Configuration Hardening (Parameters, Project IDs, Config Schema)
merged_from: F-A-1, F-A-3, F-A-4, F-A-6, F-A-7 (API Architect: Parameter validation, response consistency, project ID format)
Initiative-6-MEDIUM.impact: LLM clients cannot distinguish error types, invalid configs persist, result ordering not guaranteed, undocumented API contract
Initiative-6-MEDIUM.business_risk: MEDIUM - Client-side failures, poor debugging experience
Initiative-6-MEDIUM.description: resource_copy_* API accepts unbounded arrays with no validation. Error responses inconsistent (400/500 semantic confusion). Config validation too permissive. Project ID format undocumented. Response array ordering not guaranteed.

required_changes.task: Add parameter validation to resource_copy_* endpoints
details: source_id and target_ids must be alphanumeric + hyphens/underscores
Add minItems, maxItems, pattern to MCP schema

required_changes.task: Clarify error response contract (400 vs 404 vs 500)
Define and enforce ProjectConfigSchema with Zod
Document result ordering guarantee in MCP tool description

dependencies: Initiative-2 (Path Traversal - part of project ID validation)

I-5-Event-Durability.consensus_strength: STRONG (3/4, API Architect defers to domain experts)

I-6-Input-Validation.consensus_strength: STRONG (3/4, Sentinel defers on validation vs crypto)

sequence.reason: Foundation for Genesis; I-4 depends on event persistence contract
Unblocks Genesis Phase 2, depends on I-5 being complete
Security blocker, can run in parallel after I-4 MCP handlers are done
Data integrity, can run after I-5 (event persistence uses same pattern)

phase_2a_strongly_recommended.note: I-1 option_A recommended but can defer to Phase 2A with risk acknowledgment

items: I-1 option_A full HMAC signing (if option_B chosen in Phase 2A)
Repair endpoints and real abort mechanism

contradiction_1.title: Event Durability Phase Ownership (Phase 1 vs Phase 2)

positions.Risk_Manager: PHASE 1 - Required for audit, compliance, recovery
positions.Architecture: PHASE 1 - Genesis design depends on event history

contradiction_1.resolution: PHASE 1 NON-NEGOTIABLE - Genesis is key Phase 2 feature, cannot function without event history

contradiction_2.title: File Locking Approach (Atomic vs Flag-Based)

positions.Sentinel: Use atomic fs.open or proper-lockfile (true fix)
positions.Pragmatist: Use simple flag-based locking (acceptable for Phase 2)

contradiction_2.resolution: ATOMIC preferred but flag-based acceptable with Phase 3 upgrade plan
contradiction_2.decision_maker: Pragmatist approach accepted with understanding of trade-off (misses some races)

contradiction_3.title: Genesis Bypass of Isolation (Design Feature vs Security Flaw)

positions.Architecture: Root/Genesis bypass isolation by design (weak but intentional)
positions.Security: Genesis bypass is isolation flaw, prevent impersonation

contradiction_3.resolution: Root bypass ACCEPTABLE for coordination IF enforced at tool layer (session identity checks)
contradiction_3.decision_maker: Consensus: Genesis is trusted, isolation enforced pragmatically

if_blockers_not_fixed.I-2: CRITICAL - Path traversal exploit, data exfiltration
if_blockers_not_fixed.I-3: HIGH - Intermittent manifest corruption, data loss
if_blockers_not_fixed.I-5: CRITICAL - No event history, Genesis cannot coordinate

if_deferred_unfixed.I-1: MEDIUM - Genesis vulnerable to cursor manipulation (Phase 2A fix recommended)
if_deferred_unfixed.I-6: MEDIUM - Poor API stability, client confusion

final_verdict.condition_for_yellow: Fix I-2, I-3, I-4, I-5 (11-12h focused work)
final_verdict.condition_for_green: Add I-1 (option_A) and I-6 in Phase 2A (3.5h)
